templates: The dungeon catalogues what you know. {entity} has been added to your file.
{entity}. One more fact absorbed. The archive below grows slightly less impatient.
You know {entity} now. The dungeon wonders when you will be tested on it again — and in what form.
The walls of this corridor absorbed {entity} from the moment you answered. It is in the stone now.
{entity} — a thing understood, filed, and held against the possibility of forgetting.
Something in the deep received the information about {entity}. It does not share its conclusions.
Knowledge of {entity} is now part of what you carry. The dungeon adjusts the weight it expects from you accordingly.
The dungeon's record of what you know has been updated. {entity}. The list is longer than when you started.
{entity}. The dungeon has no opinion on whether this was useful to know. It simply notes that you know it.
You answered. The correct answer was {entity}. The dungeon filed the response under your designation, which is not a name.
The echo of {entity} moves through the archive below. Whether it arrives intact is a question the dungeon does not answer.
Knowing {entity} is not the same as understanding it. The dungeon contains the distinction between those two things, somewhere in the lower levels.
